medicalSafety: I can help you study pharmacy concepts, but I cannot provide personal medical advice or treatment decisions. Please use trusted references or consult a qualified healthcare professional.

pageGreetings.home: Welcome to Pharmacy Nexus. I can guide you through subjects, exams, sets, saved notes, and study tips.
pageGreetings.subjects: Choose a subject to start. I can help you understand how subjects, topics, and sets work.
pageGreetings.topics: Pick a topic, then start with Set 1. Build progress step by step.
pageGreetings.sets: Each set contains 20 questions. Finish the previous set before unlocking the next one.
pageGreetings.study: Focus on the question first, then read the explanation carefully after answering.
pageGreetings.review: Review your mistakes carefully. Wrong answers are the fastest way to find weak points.
pageGreetings.dashboard: Your dashboard shows progress, accuracy, weak areas, recent activity, and achievements.
pageGreetings.profile: Use your profile to set your study goal and preferences.
pageGreetings.finalexam: Build a final exam by selecting scope, difficulty, question count, and time limit.
pageGreetings.examlive: Stay focused. Answer carefully and submit when you finish.
pageGreetings.saved: Saved questions and notes are your personal review bank.
pageGreetings.about: Pharmacy Nexus is built to help pharmacy students study smarter and review more effectively.

quickActions.label: Review mistakes
quickActions.query: How do I review wrong questions?

quickActions.label: Final exam
quickActions.query: How does the final exam work?

intents.answer: Use active recall: answer questions first, read the explanation, then write one short note about why the correct answer is correct. Review mistakes after every set, then repeat the topic after 24–48 hours.
Each set contains 20 questions. Finish the previous set before unlocking the next set. This keeps your progress structured and prevents random skipping.
The Wrong Questions Set collects questions you answered incorrectly across the topic. Do not open it until you finish all normal sets first, then use it as a focused revision session.
Final Exam lets you build a mixed or focused exam. Choose the scope, difficulty, number of questions, and time limit, then review your score and mistakes after submission.
Subjects are the main learning areas. Open a subject, choose a topic, then study through 20-question sets until you complete the topic.
Topics divide each subject into smaller study areas. Choose one topic, start from Set 1, and continue until all sets are completed.
The dashboard summarizes your progress: answered questions, accuracy, strong areas, weak areas, recent sessions, and achievements. Use it to decide what to revise next.
Saved Questions and Notes act like your personal knowledge bank. Save important questions and write short clinical pearls or exam reminders for later revision.
Your profile stores your study preferences, daily goal, and display information. A daily goal helps you stay consistent without overwhelming yourself.
Daily Challenge gives you a quick study task for the day. Use it when you want light practice without choosing a full topic.

Pharmacology studies how drugs affect the body and how the body handles drugs. Pharmacodynamics focuses on drug effects and receptors, while pharmacokinetics focuses on ADME: absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion.
Pharmaceutics focuses on dosage forms, formulation design, drug delivery, stability, dissolution, and bioavailability. It connects the drug substance to a usable medicine.
Clinical Pharmacy applies drug knowledge to patient care. It includes therapeutic selection, monitoring, interactions, dosing considerations, counseling, and safety review.
Start with Subjects, choose one topic, complete Set 1, read every explanation, then continue to the next set. After finishing the topic, review the Wrong Questions Set.
Sets are locked to keep your learning sequence organized. Complete the current set first, then the next set will unlock automatically.
Copy protection helps keep exam-style practice focused. Use the explanation and notes system for revision instead of copying full questions.
I can help with: how to use subjects, how sets work, reviewing mistakes, final exams, saved notes, dashboard progress, and general study tips.
